tEn: ELECTRA: Pre-training Text Encoders as Discriminators Rather Than Generators
tZh: ELECTRA：将文本编码器预训练为判别器而非生成器
sEn: ELECTRA proposes a more sample-efficient pre-training task called replaced token detection, outperforming BERT and matching RoBERTa/XLNet with less compute.
sZh: ELECTRA提出了一种更高效的预训练任务——替换令牌检测，在相同计算量下优于BERT，并以少于1/4的计算量达到RoBERTa和XLNet的性能。
absEn: Masked language modeling (MLM) pre-training methods such as BERT corrupt the input by replacing some tokens with [MASK] and then train a model to reconstruct the original tokens. While they produce good results when transferred to downstream NLP tasks, they generally require large amounts of compute to be effective. As an alternative, we propose a more sample-efficient pre-training task called replaced token detection. Instead of masking the input, our approach corrupts it by replacing some tokens with plausible alternatives sampled from a small generator network. Then, instead of training a model that predicts the original identities of the corrupted tokens, we train a discriminative model that predicts whether each token in the corrupted input was replaced by a generator sample or not. Thorough experiments demonstrate this new pre-training task is more efficient than MLM because the task is defined over all input tokens rather than just the small subset that was masked out. As a result, the contextual representations learned by our approach substantially outperform the ones learned by BERT given the same model size, data, and compute. The gains are particularly strong for small models; for example, we train a model on one GPU for 4 days that outperforms GPT (trained using 30x more compute) on the GLUE natural language understanding benchmark. Our approach also works well at scale, where it performs comparably to RoBERTa and XLNet while using less than 1/4 of their compute and outperforms them when using the same amount of compute.
absZh: 掩码语言建模（MLM）预训练方法（如BERT）通过用[MASK]替换部分令牌来破坏输入，然后训练模型重建原始令牌。虽然这些方法在迁移到下游NLP任务时表现良好，但通常需要大量计算才能有效。作为替代，我们提出了一种更高效的预训练任务，称为替换令牌检测。我们的方法不是掩码输入，而是通过从小型生成器网络中采样合理的替代令牌来替换部分令牌，从而破坏输入。然后，我们不训练模型预测被破坏令牌的原始身份，而是训练一个判别模型，预测被破坏输入中的每个令牌是否被生成器样本替换。大量实验表明，这种新的预训练任务比MLM更高效，因为该任务定义在所有输入令牌上，而不仅仅是掩码掉的一小部分。因此，在相同模型大小、数据和计算量下，我们的方法学习到的上下文表示显著优于BERT。对于小模型，增益尤为显著；例如，我们在一个GPU上训练4天的模型，在GLUE自然语言理解基准测试中超越了使用30倍计算量训练的GPT。我们的方法在大规模下也表现良好，使用不到1/4的计算量即可与RoBERTa和XLNet相媲美，并在相同计算量下超越它们。

contrib.en: Proposes replaced token detection, a more sample-efficient pre-training task than masked language modeling.
contrib.zh: 提出替换词检测，一种比掩码语言建模更高效的预训练任务。

contrib.en: Trains a discriminative model on all input tokens, not just a masked subset, improving compute efficiency.
contrib.zh: 对所有输入词元训练判别模型，而非仅掩码子集，提升计算效率。

contrib.en: Demonstrates ELECTRA outperforms BERT, GPT, RoBERTa, and XLNet under same compute or model size.
contrib.zh: 证明ELECTRA在相同算力或模型规模下优于BERT、GPT、RoBERTa和XLNet。

contrib.en: Shows strong gains for small models, e.g., ELECTRA-Small beats GPT on GLUE with 30x less compute.
contrib.zh: 小模型表现突出，如ELECTRA-Small在GLUE上以30倍更少算力超越GPT。

contrib.en: Achieves state-of-the-art on SQuAD 2.0 with ELECTRA-Large while using less compute than prior methods.
contrib.zh: ELECTRA-Large在SQuAD 2.0上达到最优，且算力少于先前方法。

contrib.en: Introduces weight sharing and smaller generators to further improve efficiency and performance.
contrib.zh: 引入权重共享和更小生成器以进一步提升效率和性能。

limits.en: Evaluation limited to English text; multilingual applicability not explored.
limits.zh: 评估仅限于英文文本，未探索多语言适用性。

limits.en: Generator trained with maximum likelihood, not adversarially, limiting potential gains.
limits.zh: 生成器使用最大似然训练而非对抗训练，限制了潜在收益。

limits.en: Two-stage training and adversarial training underperform joint training, showing limited improvement.
limits.zh: 两阶段训练和对抗训练不如联合训练，改进有限。

limits.en: Large-scale comparisons not fully apples-to-apples due to different training data and hyperparameters.
limits.zh: 大规模对比因训练数据和超参数不同而不完全公平。

limits.en: Parameter efficiency not fully explained; more analysis needed for complete understanding.
limits.zh: 参数效率未完全解释，需更多分析以全面理解。

items.en: Masked language modeling (MLM) pre-training methods such as BERT corrupt the input by replacing some tokens with [MASK] and then train a model to reconstruct the original tokens. While they produce good results when transferred to downstream NLP tasks, they generally require large amounts of compute to be effective. As an alternative, we propose a more sample-efficient pre-training task called replaced token detection. Instead of masking the input, our approach corrupts it by replacing some tokens with plausible alternatives sampled from a small generator network. Then, instead of training a model that predicts the original identities of the corrupted tokens, we train a discriminative model that predicts whether each token in the corrupted input was replaced by a generator sample or not. Thorough experiments demonstrate this new pre-training task is more efficient than MLM because the task is defined over all input tokens rather than just the small subset that was masked out. As a result, the contextual representations learned by our approach substantially outperform the ones learned by BERT given the same model size, data, and compute. The gains are particularly strong for small models; for example, we train a model on one GPU for 4 days that outperforms GPT (trained using 30x more compute) on the GLUE natural language understanding benchmark. Our approach also works well at scale, where it performs comparably to RoBERTa and XLNet while using less than 1/4 of their compute and outperforms them when using the same amount of compute.
items.zh: 诸如 BERT 等掩码语言建模（MLM）预训练方法通过用 [MASK] 替换部分 token 来破坏输入，然后训练模型重建原始 token。虽然这些方法在迁移到下游 NLP 任务时能产生良好结果，但它们通常需要大量算力才能有效。作为替代，我们提出了一种更样本高效的预训练任务，称为替换 token 检测。我们的方法不是掩码输入，而是用从小型生成器网络中采样的合理替代 token 替换部分 token 来破坏输入。然后，我们不训练模型预测被破坏 token 的原始身份，而是训练一个判别模型，预测被破坏输入中的每个 token 是否被生成器样本替换。大量实验表明，这种新的预训练任务比 MLM 更高效，因为该任务定义在所有输入 token 上，而不仅仅是掩码掉的一小部分。因此，在相同模型大小、数据和算力下，我们的方法学习到的上下文表示显著优于 BERT 学习到的表示。对于小型模型，增益尤为显著；例如，我们在一个 GPU 上训练 4 天的模型在 GLUE 自然语言理解基准上优于 GPT（使用 30 倍算力训练）。我们的方法在大规模下也表现良好，在使用不到 1/4 算力的情况下与 RoBERTa 和 XLNet 性能相当，并在使用相同算力时优于它们。

items.en: Current state-of-the-art representation learning methods for language can be viewed as learning denoising autoencoders (Vincent et al., 2008). They select a small subset of the unlabeled input sequence (typically 15%), mask the identities of those tokens (e.g., BERT; Devlin et al. (2019)) or attention to those tokens (e.g., XLNet; Yang et al. (2019)), and then train the network to recover the original input. While more effective than conventional language-model pre-training due to learning bidirectional representations, these masked language modeling (MLM) approaches incur a substantial compute cost because the network only learns from 15% of the tokens per example.
items.zh: 当前最先进的表示学习方法可以视为学习去噪自编码器（Vincent 等人，2008）。它们选择一小部分未标记输入序列（通常为 15%），掩码这些 token 的身份（例如 BERT；Devlin 等人（2019））或对这些 token 的注意力（例如 XLNet；Yang 等人（2019）），然后训练网络恢复原始输入。虽然由于学习双向表示而比传统语言模型预训练更有效，但这些掩码语言建模（MLM）方法会带来大量算力成本，因为网络每个样本仅从 15% 的 token 中学习。

items.en: As an alternative, we propose replaced token detection, a pre-training task in which the model learns to distinguish real input tokens from plausible but synthetically generated replacements. Instead of masking, our method corrupts the input by replacing some tokens with samples from a proposal distribution, which is typically the output of a small masked language model. This corruption procedure solves a mismatch in BERT (although not in XLNet) where the network sees artificial [MASK] tokens during pre-training but not when being fine-tuned on downstream tasks. We then pre-train the network as a discriminator that predicts for every token whether it is an original or a replacement. In contrast, MLM trains the network as a generator that predicts the original identities of the corrupted tokens. A key advantage of our discriminative task is that the model learns from all input tokens instead of just the small masked-out subset, making it more computationally efficient. Although our approach is reminiscent of training the discriminator of a GAN, our method is not adversarial in that the generator producing corrupted tokens is trained with maximum likelihood due to the difficulty of applying GANs to text (Caccia et al., 2018).
items.zh: 作为替代，我们提出替换 token 检测，这是一种预训练任务，模型学习区分真实输入 token 与合理但合成生成的替换 token。我们的方法不是掩码，而是用来自提议分布（通常是小型掩码语言模型的输出）的样本替换部分 token 来破坏输入。这种破坏过程解决了 BERT（但 XLNet 没有）中的一个不匹配问题，即网络在预训练期间看到人工 [MASK] token，但在下游任务微调时却看不到。然后，我们将网络预训练为一个判别器，预测每个 token 是原始的还是替换的。相比之下，MLM 将网络训练为生成器，预测被破坏 token 的原始身份。我们判别任务的一个关键优势是模型从所有输入 token 中学习，而不仅仅是掩码掉的一小部分，这使得计算效率更高。虽然我们的方法让人联想到 GAN 的判别器训练，但由于将 GAN 应用于文本的困难（Caccia 等人，2018），我们的方法不是对抗性的，生成被破坏 token 的生成器使用最大似然训练。

items.en: We call our approach ELECTRA111Code and pre-trained weights will be released at https://github.com/google-research/electra for “Efficiently Learning an Encoder that Classifies Token Replacements Accurately.” As in prior work, we apply it to pre-train Transformer text encoders (Vaswani et al., 2017) that can be fine-tuned on downstream tasks. Through a series of ablations, we show that learning from all input positions causes ELECTRA to train much faster than BERT. We also show ELECTRA achieves higher accuracy on downstream tasks when fully trained.
items.zh: 我们将我们的方法称为 ELECTRA，意为“高效学习准确分类 token 替换的编码器”。与先前工作一样，我们将其应用于预训练 Transformer 文本编码器（Vaswani 等人，2017），这些编码器可以在下游任务上微调。通过一系列消融实验，我们表明从所有输入位置学习使 ELECTRA 的训练速度远快于 BERT。我们还表明，在完全训练后，ELECTRA 在下游任务上实现了更高的准确率。

items.en: Most current pre-training methods require large amounts of compute to be effective, raising concerns about their cost and accessibility. Since pre-training with more compute almost always results in better downstream accuracies, we argue an important consideration for pre-training methods should be compute efficiency as well as absolute downstream performance. From this viewpoint, we train ELECTRA models of various sizes and evaluate their downstream performance vs. their compute requirement. In particular, we run experiments on the GLUE natural language understanding benchmark (Wang et al., 2019) and SQuAD question answering benchmark (Rajpurkar et al., 2016). ELECTRA substantially outperforms MLM-based methods such as BERT and XLNet given the same model size, data, and compute (see Figure 1). For example, we build an ELECTRA-Small model that can be trained on 1 GPU in 4 days.222It has 1/20th the parameters and requires 1/135th the pre-training compute of BERT-Large. ELECTRA-Small outperforms a comparably small BERT model by 5 points on GLUE, and even outperforms the much larger GPT model (Radford et al., 2018). Our approach also works well at large scale, where we train an ELECTRA-Large model that performs comparably to RoBERTa (Liu et al., 2019) and XLNet (Yang et al., 2019), despite having fewer parameters and using 1/4 of the compute for training. Training ELECTRA-Large further results in an even stronger model that outperforms ALBERT (Lan et al., 2019) on GLUE and sets a new state-of-the-art for SQuAD 2.0. Taken together, our results indicate that the discriminative task of distinguishing real data from challenging negative samples is more compute-efficient and parameter-efficient than existing generative approaches for language representation learning.
items.zh: 大多数当前的预训练方法需要大量算力才能有效，这引发了对其成本和可访问性的担忧。由于更多算力的预训练几乎总是带来更好的下游准确率，我们认为预训练方法的一个重要考虑因素应该是计算效率以及绝对的下游性能。从这个角度出发，我们训练了各种大小的 ELECTRA 模型，并评估其下游性能与算力需求。具体来说，我们在 GLUE 自然语言理解基准（Wang 等人，2019）和 SQuAD 问答基准（Rajpurkar 等人，2016）上进行了实验。在相同模型大小、数据和算力下，ELECTRA 显著优于基于 MLM 的方法，如 BERT 和 XLNet（见图 1）。例如，我们构建了一个 ELECTRA-Small 模型，可以在 1 个 GPU 上训练 4 天。ELECTRA-Small 在 GLUE 上比同等大小的 BERT 模型高出 5 分，甚至优于大得多的 GPT 模型（Radford 等人，2018）。我们的方法在大规模下也表现良好，我们训练了一个 ELECTRA-Large 模型，其性能与 RoBERTa（Liu 等人，2019）和 XLNet（Yang 等人，2019）相当，尽管参数更少且训练算力仅为 1/4。进一步训练 ELECTRA-Large 得到了一个更强的模型，在 GLUE 上优于 ALBERT（Lan 等人，2019），并在 SQuAD 2.0 上创造了新的最先进水平。综合来看，我们的结果表明，区分真实数据与具有挑战性的负样本的判别任务在计算效率和参数效率上优于现有的语言表示学习生成方法。

items.capEn: Figure 1: Replaced token detection pre-training consistently outperforms masked language model pre-training given the same compute budget. The left figure is a zoomed-in view of the dashed box.
items.capZh: 图 1：在相同算力预算下，替换 token 检测预训练始终优于掩码语言模型预训练。左图是虚线框的放大视图。

items.capEn: Figure 2: An overview of replaced token detection. The generator can be any model that produces an output distribution over tokens, but we usually use a small masked language model that is trained jointly with the discriminator. Although the models are structured like in a GAN, we train the generator with maximum likelihood rather than adversarially due to the difficulty of applying GANs to text. After pre-training, we throw out the generator and only fine-tune the discriminator (the ELECTRA model) on downstream tasks.
items.capZh: 图 2：替换 token 检测概述。生成器可以是任何产生 token 输出分布的模型，但我们通常使用一个与判别器联合训练的小型掩码语言模型。虽然模型结构类似于 GAN，但由于将 GAN 应用于文本的困难，我们使用最大似然而非对抗性训练生成器。预训练后，我们丢弃生成器，仅在下游任务上微调判别器（ELECTRA 模型）。

items.en: We first describe the replaced token detection pre-training task; see Figure 2 for an overview. We suggest and evaluate several modeling improvements for this method in Section 3.2.
items.zh: 我们首先描述替换 token 检测预训练任务；参见图 2 的概述。我们在第 3.2 节中提出并评估了该方法的若干建模改进。

items.en: Our approach trains two neural networks, a generator G𝐺G and a discriminator D𝐷D. Each one primarily consists of an encoder (e.g., a Transformer network) that maps a sequence on input tokens 𝒙=[x1,…,xn]𝒙subscript𝑥1…subscript𝑥𝑛\bm{x}=[x_{1},...,x_{n}] into a sequence of contextualized vector representations h​(𝒙)=[h1,…,hn]ℎ𝒙subscriptℎ1…subscriptℎ𝑛h(\bm{x})=[h_{1},...,h_{n}]. For a given position t𝑡t, (in our case only positions where xt=[MASK]subscript𝑥𝑡[MASK]x_{t}=\texttt{[MASK]}), the generator outputs a probability for generating a particular token xtsubscript𝑥𝑡x_{t} with a softmax layer:
items.zh: 我们的方法训练两个神经网络：生成器 G 和判别器 D。每个网络主要由一个编码器（例如 Transformer 网络）组成，该编码器将输入 token 序列 x = [x1, ..., xn] 映射为上下文向量表示序列 h(x) = [h1, ..., hn]。对于给定位置 t（在我们的情况下，仅当 xt = [MASK] 时），生成器通过 softmax 层输出生成特定 token xt 的概率：

items.en: where e𝑒e denotes token embeddings. For a given position t𝑡t, the discriminator predicts whether the token xtsubscript𝑥𝑡x_{t} is “real,” i.e., that it comes from the data rather than the generator distribution, with a sigmoid output layer:
items.zh: 其中 e 表示 token 嵌入。对于给定位置 t，判别器通过 sigmoid 输出层预测 token xt 是否为“真实”，即它来自数据而非生成器分布：

items.en: The generator is trained to perform masked language modeling (MLM). Given an input 𝒙=[x1,x2,…,xn]𝒙subscript𝑥1subscript𝑥2…subscript𝑥𝑛\bm{x}=[x_{1},x_{2},...,x_{n}], MLM first select a random set of positions (integers between 1 and n𝑛n) to mask out 𝒎=[m1,…,mk]𝒎subscript𝑚1…subscript𝑚𝑘\bm{m}=[m_{1},...,m_{k}].333Typically k=⌈0.15​n⌉𝑘0.15𝑛k=\lceil 0.15n\rceil, i.e., 15% of the tokens are masked out. The tokens in the selected positions are replaced with a [MASK] token: we denote this as 𝒙masked=replace​(𝒙,𝒎,[MASK])superscript𝒙maskedreplace𝒙𝒎[MASK]\bm{x}^{\text{masked}}=\textsc{replace}(\bm{x},\bm{m},\texttt{[MASK]}). The generator then learns to predict the original identities of the masked-out tokens. The discriminator is trained to distinguish tokens in the data from tokens that have been replaced by generator samples. More specifically, we create a corrupted example 𝒙corruptsuperscript𝒙corrupt\bm{x}^{\text{corrupt}} by replacing the masked-out tokens with generator samples and train the discriminator to predict which tokens in 𝒙corruptsuperscript𝒙corrupt\bm{x}^{\text{corrupt}} match the original input 𝒙𝒙\bm{x}. Formally, model inputs are constructed according to
items.zh: 生成器被训练执行掩码语言建模（MLM）。给定输入 x = [x1, x2, ..., xn]，MLM 首先随机选择一组位置（1 到 n 之间的整数）进行掩码 m = [m1, ..., mk]。通常 k = ⌈0.15n⌉，即 15% 的 token 被掩码。选定位置的 token 被替换为 [MASK] token：我们将其记为 x_masked = replace(x, m, [MASK])。然后生成器学习预测被掩码 token 的原始身份。判别器被训练区分数据中的 token 与被生成器样本替换的 token。更具体地说，我们通过用生成器样本替换被掩码 token 来创建损坏样本 x_corrupt，并训练判别器预测 x_corrupt 中的哪些 token 与原始输入 x 匹配。形式上，模型输入按以下方式构建：

items.en: Although similar to the training objective of a GAN, there are several key differences. First, if the generator happens to generate the correct token, that token is considered “real” instead of “fake”; we found this formulation to moderately improve results on downstream tasks. More importantly, the generator is trained with maximum likelihood rather than being trained adversarially to fool the discriminator. Adversarially training the generator is challenging because it is impossible to back-propagate through sampling from the generator. Although we experimented circumventing this issue by using reinforcement learning to train the generator (see Appendix F), this performed worse than maximum-likelihood training. Lastly, we do not supply the generator with a noise vector as input, as is typical with a GAN.
items.zh: 虽然类似于 GAN 的训练目标，但存在几个关键区别。首先，如果生成器恰好生成了正确的 token，则该 token 被视为“真实”而非“虚假”；我们发现这种表述对下游任务的结果有适度改善。更重要的是，生成器使用最大似然训练，而不是对抗性地训练以欺骗判别器。对抗性训练生成器具有挑战性，因为无法通过从生成器采样进行反向传播。虽然我们尝试通过使用强化学习训练生成器来规避此问题（参见附录 F），但其表现不如最大似然训练。最后，我们不向生成器提供噪声向量作为输入，而这是 GAN 的典型做法。

items.en: over a large corpus 𝒳𝒳\mathcal{X} of raw text. We approximate the expectations in the losses with a single sample. We don’t back-propagate the discriminator loss through the generator (indeed, we can’t because of the sampling step). After pre-training, we throw out the generator and fine-tune the discriminator on downstream tasks.
items.zh: 在原始文本的大语料库 X 上。我们用单个样本近似损失中的期望。我们不通过生成器反向传播判别器损失（实际上，由于采样步骤，我们无法做到）。预训练后，我们丢弃生成器，并在下游任务上微调判别器。

items.en: We evaluate on the General Language Understanding Evaluation (GLUE) benchmark (Wang et al., 2019) and Stanford Question Answering (SQuAD) dataset (Rajpurkar et al., 2016). GLUE contains a variety of tasks covering textual entailment (RTE and MNLI) question-answer entailment (QNLI), paraphrase (MRPC), question paraphrase (QQP), textual similarity (STS), sentiment (SST), and linguistic acceptability (CoLA). See Appendix C for more details on the GLUE tasks. Our evaluation metrics are Spearman correlation for STS, Matthews correlation for CoLA, and accuracy for the other GLUE tasks; we generally report the average score over all tasks. For SQuAD, we evaluate on versions 1.1, in which models select the span of text answering a question, and 2.0, in which some questions are unanswerable by the passage. We use the standard evaluation metrics of Exact-Match (EM) and F1 scores. For most experiments we pre-train on the same data as BERT, which consists of 3.3 Billion tokens from Wikipedia and BooksCorpus (Zhu et al., 2015). However, for our Large model we pre-trained on the data used for XLNet (Yang et al., 2019), which extends the BERT dataset to 33B tokens by including data from ClueWeb (Callan et al., 2009), CommonCrawl, and Gigaword (Parker et al., 2011). All of the pre-training and evaluation is on English data, although we think it would be interesting to apply our methods to multilingual data in the future.
items.zh: 我们在通用语言理解评估（GLUE）基准（Wang 等人，2019）和斯坦福问答（SQuAD）数据集（Rajpurkar 等人，2016）上进行评估。GLUE 包含多种任务，涵盖文本蕴含（RTE 和 MNLI）、问答蕴含（QNLI）、释义（MRPC）、问题释义（QQP）、文本相似度（STS）、情感分析（SST）和语言可接受性（CoLA）。有关 GLUE 任务的更多详细信息，请参见附录 C。我们的评估指标是 STS 的 Spearman 相关性、CoLA 的 Matthews 相关性以及其他 GLUE 任务的准确率；我们通常报告所有任务的平均得分。对于 SQuAD，我们在 1.1 版本（模型选择回答问题的文本跨度）和 2.0 版本（某些问题无法由段落回答）上进行评估。我们使用标准评估指标：精确匹配（EM）和 F1 分数。对于大多数实验，我们在与 BERT 相同的数据上进行预训练，该数据包含来自 Wikipedia 和 BooksCorpus 的 33 亿 token（Zhu 等人，2015）。然而，对于我们的 Large 模型，我们在 XLNet（Yang 等人，2019）使用的数据上进行预训练，该数据通过包含来自 ClueWeb（Callan 等人，2009）、CommonCrawl 和 Gigaword（Parker 等人，2011）的数据将 BERT 数据集扩展到 330 亿 token。所有预训练和评估均使用英语数据，尽管我们认为将来将我们的方法应用于多语言数据会很有趣。

items.en: Our model architecture and most hyperparameters are the same as BERT’s. For fine-tuning on GLUE, we add simple linear classifiers on top of ELECTRA. For SQuAD, we add the question-answering module from XLNet on top of ELECTRA, which is slightly more sophisticated than BERT’s in that it jointly rather than independently predicts the start and end positions and has a “answerability” classifier added for SQuAD 2.0. Some of our evaluation datasets are small, which means accuracies of fine-tuned models can vary substantially depending on the random seed. We therefore report the median of 10 fine-tuning runs from the same pre-trained checkpoint for each result. Unless stated otherwise, results are on the dev set. See the appendix for further training details and hyperparameter values.
items.zh: 我们的模型架构和大多数超参数与 BERT 相同。对于 GLUE 上的微调，我们在 ELECTRA 之上添加简单的线性分类器。对于 SQuAD，我们在 ELECTRA 之上添加 XLNet 的问答模块，该模块比 BERT 的模块稍复杂，因为它联合而非独立地预测起始和结束位置，并为 SQuAD 2.0 添加了“可回答性”分类器。我们的一些评估数据集很小，这意味着微调模型的准确率可能因随机种子而有很大差异。因此，我们报告每个结果来自同一预训练检查点的 10 次微调运行的中位数。除非另有说明，结果均在开发集上。有关更多训练细节和超参数值，请参见附录。

items.en: We improve our method by proposing and evaluating several extensions to the model. Unless stated otherwise, these experiments use the same model size and training data as BERT-Base.
items.zh: 我们通过提出并评估模型的若干扩展来改进我们的方法。除非另有说明，这些实验使用与 BERT-Base 相同的模型大小和训练数据。

items.en: Weight Sharing We propose improving the efficiency of the pre-training by sharing weights between the generator and discriminator. If the generator and discriminator are the same size, all of the transformer weights can be tied. However, we found it to be more efficient to have a small generator, in which case we only share the embeddings (both the token and positional embeddings) of the generator and discriminator. In this case we use embeddings the size of the discriminator’s hidden states.444We add linear layers to the generator to project the embeddings into generator-hidden-sized representations. The “input” and “output” token embeddings of the generator are always tied as in BERT.
items.zh: 权重共享 我们提出通过在生成器和判别器之间共享权重来提高预训练效率。如果生成器和判别器大小相同，则可以绑定所有 Transformer 权重。然而，我们发现使用小型生成器更高效，在这种情况下，我们仅共享生成器和判别器的嵌入（包括 token 嵌入和位置嵌入）。在这种情况下，我们使用与判别器隐藏状态大小相同的嵌入。我们添加线性层将生成器的嵌入投影为生成器隐藏大小的表示。生成器的“输入”和“输出” token 嵌入始终像 BERT 中一样绑定。

items.en: We compare the weight tying strategies when the generator is the same size as the discriminator. We train these models for 500k steps. GLUE scores are 83.6 for no weight tying, 84.3 for tying token embeddings, and 84.4 for tying all weights. We hypothesize that ELECTRA benefits from tied token embeddings because masked language modeling is particularly effective at learning these representations: while the discriminator only updates tokens that are present in the input or are sampled by the generator, the generator’s softmax over the vocabulary densely updates all token embeddings. On the other hand, tying all encoder weights caused little improvement while incurring the significant disadvantage of requiring the generator and discriminator to be the same size. Based on these findings, we use tied embeddings for further experiments in this paper.
items.zh: 我们比较了生成器与判别器大小相同时的权重绑定策略。我们训练这些模型 500k 步。GLUE 得分为：无权重绑定 83.6，绑定 token 嵌入 84.3，绑定所有权重 84.4。我们假设 ELECTRA 受益于绑定的 token 嵌入，因为掩码语言建模在学习这些表示方面特别有效：虽然判别器仅更新输入中存在或被生成器采样的 token，但生成器在词汇表上的 softmax 密集地更新所有 token 嵌入。另一方面，绑定所有编码器权重几乎没有改进，同时带来了要求生成器和判别器大小相同的显著缺点。基于这些发现，我们在本文的进一步实验中使用绑定的嵌入。

items.en: Smaller Generators If the generator and discriminator are the same size, training ELECTRA would take around twice as much compute per step as training only with masked language modeling. We suggest using a smaller generator to reduce this factor. Specifically, we make models smaller by decreasing the layer sizes while keeping the other hyperparameters constant. We also explore using an extremely simple “unigram” generator that samples fake tokens according their frequency in the train corpus. GLUE scores for differently-sized generators and discriminators are shown in the left of Figure 3. All models are trained for 500k steps, which puts the smaller generators at a disadvantage in terms of compute because they require less compute per training step. Nevertheless, we find that models work best with generators 1/4-1/2 the size of the discriminator. We speculate that having too strong of a generator may pose a too-challenging task for the discriminator, preventing it from learning as effectively. In particular, the discriminator may have to use many of its parameters modeling the generator rather than the actual data distribution. Further experiments in this paper use the best generator size found for the given discriminator size.
items.zh: 更小的生成器 如果生成器和判别器大小相同，训练 ELECTRA 每步所需的算力大约是仅使用掩码语言建模训练的两倍。我们建议使用更小的生成器来减少这一因素。具体来说，我们通过减小层大小同时保持其他超参数不变来使模型更小。我们还探索使用一个极其简单的“unigram”生成器，根据训练语料中的频率采样虚假 token。不同大小生成器和判别器的 GLUE 得分如图 3 左侧所示。所有模型训练 500k 步，这使较小的生成器在算力方面处于劣势，因为它们每步训练所需的算力更少。尽管如此，我们发现当生成器大小为判别器的 1/4 到 1/2 时模型效果最佳。我们推测，过强的生成器可能给判别器带来过于困难的任务，阻碍其有效学习。特别是，判别器可能不得不使用许多参数来建模生成器，而不是实际的数据分布。本文的进一步实验使用针对给定判别器大小找到的最佳生成器大小。

items.capEn: Figure 3: Left: GLUE scores for different generator/discriminator sizes (number of hidden units). Interestingly, having a generator smaller than the discriminator improves results. Right: Comparison of different training algorithms. As our focus is on efficiency, the x-axis shows FLOPs rather than train steps (e.g., ELECTRA is trained for fewer steps than BERT because it includes the generator).
items.capZh: 图 3：左图：不同生成器/判别器大小（隐藏单元数）的 GLUE 得分。有趣的是，生成器小于判别器会改善结果。右图：不同训练算法的比较。由于我们关注效率，x 轴显示 FLOPs 而非训练步数（例如，ELECTRA 训练步数少于 BERT，因为它包含生成器）。

items.en: Training Algorithms Lastly, we explore other training algorithms for ELECTRA, although these did not end up improving results. The proposed training objective jointly trains the generator and discriminator. We experiment with instead using the following two-stage training procedure:
items.zh: 训练算法 最后，我们探索了ELECTRA的其他训练算法，尽管这些算法最终并未提升结果。提出的训练目标联合训练生成器和判别器。我们尝试使用以下两阶段训练过程：

items.en: Train only the generator with ℒMLMsubscriptℒMLM\mathcal{L}_{\text{MLM}} for n𝑛n steps.
items.zh: 仅使用ℒMLM训练生成器n步。

items.en: Initialize the weights of the discriminator with the weights of the generator. Then train the discriminator with ℒDiscsubscriptℒDisc\mathcal{L}_{\text{Disc}} for n𝑛n steps, keeping the generator’s weights frozen.
items.zh: 用生成器的权重初始化判别器的权重。然后使用ℒDisc训练判别器n步，保持生成器的权重冻结。

items.en: Note that the weight initialization in this procedure requires having the same size for the generator and discriminator. We found that without the weight initialization the discriminator would sometimes fail to learn at all beyond the majority class, perhaps because the generator started so far ahead of the discriminator. Joint training on the other hand naturally provides a curriculum for the discriminator where the generator starts off weak but gets better throughout training. We also explored training the generator adversarially as in a GAN, using reinforcement learning to accommodate the discrete operations of sampling from the generator. See Appendix F for details.
items.zh: 注意，此过程中的权重初始化要求生成器和判别器具有相同的大小。我们发现，如果没有权重初始化，判别器有时会完全无法学习，除了多数类，可能是因为生成器一开始就远远领先于判别器。另一方面，联合训练为判别器自然提供了一个课程，其中生成器在训练初期较弱，但在整个训练过程中逐渐变强。我们还探索了像GAN中那样对抗性训练生成器，使用强化学习来适应从生成器采样的离散操作。详见附录F。

items.en: Results are shown in the right of Figure 3. During two-stage training, downstream task performance notably improves after the switch from the generative to the discriminative objective, but does not end up outscoring joint training. Although still outperforming BERT, we found adversarial training to underperform maximum-likelihood training. Further analysis suggests the gap is caused by two problems with adversarial training. First, the adversarial generator is simply worse at masked language modeling; it achieves 58% accuracy at masked language modeling compared to 65% accuracy for an MLE-trained one. We believe the worse accuracy is mainly due to the poor sample efficiency of reinforcement learning when working in the large action space of generating text. Secondly, the adversarially trained generator produces a low-entropy output distribution where most of the probability mass is on a single token, which means there is not much diversity in the generator samples. Both of these problems have been observed in GANs for text in prior work (Caccia et al., 2018).
items.zh: 结果如图3右侧所示。在两阶段训练中，从生成目标切换到判别目标后，下游任务性能显著提升，但最终并未超过联合训练。尽管仍优于BERT，我们发现对抗性训练的表现不如最大似然训练。进一步分析表明，差距是由对抗性训练的两个问题造成的。首先，对抗性生成器在掩码语言建模方面表现更差；它在掩码语言建模上的准确率为58%，而MLE训练的生成器为65%。我们认为准确率较低主要是由于在生成文本的大动作空间中强化学习的样本效率低下。其次，对抗性训练的生成器产生低熵输出分布，其中大部分概率质量集中在单个词元上，这意味着生成器样本的多样性不足。这两个问题在先前的文本GAN工作中都已被观察到（Caccia等人，2018）。

items.en: As a goal of this work is to improve the efficiency of pre-training, we develop a small model that can be quickly trained on a single GPU. Starting with the BERT-Base hyperparameters, we shortened the sequence length (from 512 to 128), reduced the batch size (from 256 to 128), reduced the model’s hidden dimension size (from 768 to 256), and used smaller token embeddings (from 768 to 128). To provide a fair comparison, we also train a BERT-Small model using the same hyperparameters. We train BERT-Small for 1.5M steps, so it uses the same training FLOPs as ELECTRA-Small, which was trained for 1M steps.555ELECTRA requires more FLOPs per step because it consists of the generator as well as the discriminator. In addition to BERT, we compare against two less resource-intensive pre-training methods based on language modeling: ELMo (Peters et al., 2018) and GPT (Radford et al., 2018).666GPT is similar in size to BERT-Base, but is trained for fewer steps. We also show results for a base-sized ELECTRA model comparable to BERT-Base.
items.zh: 由于本工作的目标之一是提高预训练的效率，我们开发了一个可以在单个GPU上快速训练的小模型。从BERT-Base超参数开始，我们缩短了序列长度（从512到128），减小了批量大小（从256到128），减小了模型的隐藏维度大小（从768到256），并使用了更小的词元嵌入（从768到128）。为了公平比较，我们还使用相同的超参数训练了一个BERT-Small模型。我们训练BERT-Small 150万步，因此它使用的训练FLOPs与训练了100万步的ELECTRA-Small相同。555ELECTRA每步需要更多FLOPs，因为它包含生成器和判别器。除了BERT，我们还与两种基于语言建模的资源密集度较低的预训练方法进行了比较：ELMo（Peters等人，2018）和GPT（Radford等人，2018）。666GPT的大小与BERT-Base相似，但训练步数较少。我们还展示了一个与BERT-Base大小相当的base-sized ELECTRA模型的结果。

items.en: Results are shown in Table 1. See Appendix D for additional results, including stronger small-sized and base-sized models trained with more compute. ELECTRA-Small performs remarkably well given its size, achieving a higher GLUE score than other methods using substantially more compute and parameters. For example, it scores 5 points higher than a comparable BERT-Small model and even outperforms the much larger GPT model. ELECTRA-Small is trained mostly to convergence, with models trained for even less time (as little as 6 hours) still achieving reasonable performance. While small models distilled from larger pre-trained transformers can also achieve good GLUE scores (Sun et al., 2019b; Jiao et al., 2019), these models require first expending substantial compute to pre-train the larger teacher model. The results also demonstrate the strength of ELECTRA at a moderate size; our base-sized ELECTRA model substantially outperforms BERT-Base and even outperforms BERT-Large (which gets 84.0 GLUE score). We hope ELECTRA’s ability to achieve strong results with relatively little compute will broaden the accessibility of developing and applying pre-trained models in NLP.
items.zh: 结果如表1所示。更多结果见附录D，包括使用更多算力训练的更强的小型和base-sized模型。ELECTRA-Small在其大小下表现非常出色，在GLUE得分上高于使用更多算力和参数的其他方法。例如，它比可比的BERT-Small模型高出5分，甚至超过了更大的GPT模型。ELECTRA-Small的训练基本收敛，即使训练时间更短（短至6小时）的模型也能达到合理的性能。虽然从更大的预训练Transformer中蒸馏出的小模型也能获得良好的GLUE得分（Sun等人，2019b；Jiao等人，2019），但这些模型需要首先花费大量算力预训练更大的教师模型。结果还展示了ELECTRA在中等规模下的优势；我们的base-sized ELECTRA模型显著优于BERT-Base，甚至超过了BERT-Large（GLUE得分为84.0）。我们希望ELECTRA以相对较少的算力获得强大结果的能力将拓宽NLP中预训练模型的开发和应用的普及性。

items.en: We train big ELECTRA models to measure the effectiveness of the replaced token detection pre-training task at the large scale of current state-of-the-art pre-trained Transformers. Our ELECTRA-Large models are the same size as BERT-Large but are trained for much longer. In particular, we train a model for 400k steps (ELECTRA-400K; roughly 1/4 the pre-training compute of RoBERTa) and one for 1.75M steps (ELECTRA-1.75M; similar compute to RoBERTa). We use a batch size 2048 and the XLNet pre-training data. We note that although the XLNet data is similar to the data used to train RoBERTa, the comparison is not entirely direct. As a baseline, we trained our own BERT-Large model using the same hyperparameters and training time as ELECTRA-400K.
items.zh: 我们训练大型ELECTRA模型，以衡量替换词元检测预训练任务在当前最先进预训练Transformer的大规模下的有效性。我们的ELECTRA-Large模型与BERT-Large大小相同，但训练时间更长。具体来说，我们训练了一个400k步的模型（ELECTRA-400K；大约是RoBERTa预训练算力的1/4）和一个175万步的模型（ELECTRA-1.75M；与RoBERTa算力相似）。我们使用批量大小2048和XLNet预训练数据。我们注意到，尽管XLNet数据与用于训练RoBERTa的数据相似，但比较并非完全直接。作为基线，我们使用与ELECTRA-400K相同的超参数和训练时间训练了我们自己的BERT-Large模型。

items.en: Results on the GLUE dev set are shown in Table 2. ELECTRA-400K performs comparably to RoBERTa and XLNet. However, it took less than 1/4 of the compute to train ELECTRA-400K as it did to train RoBERTa and XLNet, demonstrating that ELECTRA’s sample-efficiency gains hold at large scale. Training ELECTRA for longer (ELECTRA-1.75M) results in a model that outscores them on most GLUE tasks while still requiring less pre-training compute. Surprisingly, our baseline BERT model scores notably worse than RoBERTa-100K, suggesting our models may benefit from more hyperparameter tuning or using the RoBERTa training data. ELECTRA’s gains hold on the GLUE test set (see Table 3), although these comparisons are less apples-to-apples due to the additional tricks employed by the models (see Appendix B).
items.zh: GLUE开发集的结果如表2所示。ELECTRA-400K的表现与RoBERTa和XLNet相当。然而，训练ELECTRA-400K所需的算力不到RoBERTa和XLNet的1/4，这表明ELECTRA的样本效率优势在大规模下仍然成立。更长时间的训练（ELECTRA-1.75M）产生了一个在大多数GLUE任务上得分超过它们的模型，同时仍然需要更少的预训练算力。令人惊讶的是，我们的基线BERT模型得分明显低于RoBERTa-100K，这表明我们的模型可能受益于更多的超参数调整或使用RoBERTa训练数据。ELECTRA的优势在GLUE测试集上仍然成立（见表3），尽管由于模型使用的额外技巧（见附录B），这些比较不太直接。

items.en: Results on SQuAD are shown in Table 4. Consistent, with the GLUE results, ELECTRA scores better than masked-language-modeling-based methods given the same compute resources. For example, ELECTRA-400K outperforms RoBERTa-100k and our BERT baseline, which use similar amounts of pre-training compute. ELECTRA-400K also performs comparably to RoBERTa-500K despite using less than 1/4th of the compute. Unsurprisingly, training ELECTRA longer improves results further: ELECTRA-1.75M scores higher than previous models on the SQuAD 2.0 benchmark. ELECTRA-Base also yields strong results, scoring substantially better than BERT-Base and XLNet-Base, and even surpassing BERT-Large according to most metrics. ELECTRA generally performs better at SQuAD 2.0 than 1.1. Perhaps replaced token detection, in which the model distinguishes real tokens from plausible fakes, is particularly transferable to the answerability classification of SQuAD 2.0, in which the model must distinguish answerable questions from fake unanswerable questions.
items.zh: SQuAD的结果如表4所示。与GLUE结果一致，在相同算力资源下，ELECTRA的得分高于基于掩码语言建模的方法。例如，ELECTRA-400K优于RoBERTa-100k和我们的BERT基线，它们使用了相似的预训练算力。ELECTRA-400K的表现也与RoBERTa-500K相当，尽管使用的算力不到其1/4。不出所料，更长时间的训练进一步提升了结果：ELECTRA-1.75M在SQuAD 2.0基准测试上得分高于之前的模型。ELECTRA-Base也产生了强劲的结果，显著优于BERT-Base和XLNet-Base，甚至根据大多数指标超过了BERT-Large。ELECTRA在SQuAD 2.0上的表现通常优于1.1。也许替换词元检测（模型区分真实词元和看似合理的假词元）特别适用于SQuAD 2.0的可回答性分类，其中模型必须区分可回答的问题和虚假的不可回答的问题。

items.en: We have suggested that posing the training objective over a small subset of tokens makes masked language modeling inefficient. However, it isn’t entirely obvious that this is the case. After all, the model still receives a large number of input tokens even though it predicts only a small number of masked tokens. To better understand where the gains from ELECTRA are coming from, we compare a series of other pre-training objectives that are designed to be a set of “stepping stones” between BERT and ELECTRA.
items.zh: 我们曾提出，将训练目标限定在一小部分词元上会使掩码语言建模效率低下。然而，这并不完全明显。毕竟，模型仍然接收大量输入词元，尽管它只预测少量掩码词元。为了更好地理解ELECTRA的增益来源，我们比较了一系列其他预训练目标，这些目标被设计为BERT和ELECTRA之间的一组“垫脚石”。

items.en: ELECTRA 15%: This model is identical to ELECTRA except the discriminator loss only comes from the 15% of the tokens that were masked out of the input. In other words, the sum in the discriminator loss ℒDiscsubscriptℒDisc\mathcal{L}_{\text{Disc}} is over i∈𝒎𝑖𝒎i\in\bm{m} instead of from 1 to n𝑛n.777We also trained a discriminator that learns from a random 15% of the input tokens distinct from the subset that was originally masked out; this model performed slightly worse.
items.zh: ELECTRA 15%：该模型与ELECTRA相同，只是判别器损失仅来自输入中被掩码的15%的词元。换句话说，判别器损失ℒDisc中的求和是对i∈𝒎而不是从1到n。777我们还训练了一个判别器，它从输入中随机15%的词元（与最初掩码的子集不同）学习；该模型表现稍差。

items.en: Replace MLM: This objective is the same as masked language modeling except instead of replacing masked-out tokens with [MASK], they are replaced with tokens from a generator model. This objective tests to what extent ELECTRA’s gains come from solving the discrepancy of exposing the model to [MASK] tokens during pre-training but not fine-tuning.
items.zh: 替换MLM：该目标与掩码语言建模相同，只是掩码词元不是被替换为[MASK]，而是被替换为来自生成器模型的词元。该目标测试ELECTRA的增益在多大程度上来自解决预训练中暴露于[MASK]词元而微调中不暴露的差异。

items.en: All-Tokens MLM: Like in Replace MLM, masked tokens are replaced with generator samples. Furthermore, the model predicts the identity of all tokens in the input, not just ones that were masked out. We found it improved results to train this model with an explicit copy mechanism that outputs a copy probability D𝐷D for each token using a sigmoid layer. The model’s output distribution puts D𝐷D weight on the input token plus 1−D1𝐷1-D times the output of the MLM softmax. This model is essentially a combination of BERT and ELECTRA. Note that without generator replacements, the model would trivially learn to make predictions from the vocabulary for [MASK] tokens and copy the input for other ones.
items.zh: 所有词元MLM：与替换MLM类似，掩码词元被替换为生成器样本。此外，模型预测输入中所有词元的身份，而不仅仅是掩码的词元。我们发现，使用显式复制机制训练该模型效果更好，该机制通过sigmoid层为每个词元输出复制概率D。模型的输出分布将D权重放在输入词元上，加上1-D乘以MLM softmax的输出。该模型本质上是BERT和ELECTRA的组合。注意，如果没有生成器替换，模型会简单地学习为[MASK]词元从词汇表中进行预测，并为其他词元复制输入。

items.en: Results are shown in Table 5. First, we find that ELECTRA is greatly benefiting from having a loss defined over all input tokens rather than just a subset: ELECTRA 15% performs much worse than ELECTRA. Secondly, we find that BERT performance is being slightly harmed from the pre-train fine-tune mismatch from [MASK] tokens, as Replace MLM slightly outperforms BERT. We note that BERT (including our implementation) already includes a trick to help with the pre-train/fine-tune discrepancy: masked tokens are replaced with a random token 10% of the time and are kept the same 10% of the time. However, our results suggest these simple heuristics are insufficient to fully solve the issue. Lastly, we find that All-Tokens MLM, the generative model that makes predictions over all tokens instead of a subset, closes most of the gap between BERT and ELECTRA. In total, these results suggest a large amount of ELECTRA’s improvement can be attributed to learning from all tokens and a smaller amount can be attributed to alleviating the pre-train fine-tune mismatch.
items.zh: 结果如表5所示。首先，我们发现ELECTRA从定义在所有输入词元上的损失中获益巨大，而不仅仅是子集：ELECTRA 15%的表现远差于ELECTRA。其次，我们发现BERT的性能因[MASK]词元的预训练-微调不匹配而略有受损，因为替换MLM略优于BERT。我们注意到，BERT（包括我们的实现）已经包含了一个帮助解决预训练/微调差异的技巧：掩码词元有10%的时间被替换为随机词元，10%的时间保持不变。然而，我们的结果表明这些简单的启发式方法不足以完全解决问题。最后，我们发现所有词元MLM（一种对所有词元而非子集进行预测的生成模型）缩小了BERT和ELECTRA之间的大部分差距。总的来说，这些结果表明ELECTRA的改进很大一部分可以归因于从所有词元中学习，较小一部分可以归因于缓解预训练-微调不匹配。

items.capEn: Figure 4: Left and Center: Comparison of BERT and ELECTRA for different model sizes. Right: A small ELECTRA model converges to higher downstream accuracy than BERT, showing the improvement comes from more than just faster training.
items.capZh: 图4：左和中：不同模型大小下BERT和ELECTRA的比较。右：一个小的ELECTRA模型收敛到比BERT更高的下游准确率，表明改进不仅仅来自更快的训练。

items.en: The improvement of ELECTRA over All-Tokens MLM suggests that the ELECTRA’s gains come from more than just faster training. We study this further by comparing BERT to ELECTRA for various model sizes (see Figure 4, left). We find that the gains from ELECTRA grow larger as the models get smaller. The small models are trained fully to convergence (see Figure 4, right), showing that ELECTRA achieves higher downstream accuracy than BERT when fully trained. We speculate that ELECTRA is more parameter-efficient than BERT because it does not have to model the full distribution of possible tokens at each position, but we believe more analysis is needed to completely explain ELECTRA’s parameter efficiency.
items.zh: ELECTRA相对于所有词元MLM的改进表明，ELECTRA的增益不仅仅来自更快的训练。我们通过比较不同模型大小下BERT和ELECTRA来进一步研究这一点（见图4，左）。我们发现，随着模型变小，ELECTRA的增益变得更大。小模型完全训练到收敛（见图4，右），表明ELECTRA在完全训练时达到了比BERT更高的下游准确率。我们推测ELECTRA比BERT更具参数效率，因为它不必在每个位置建模可能词元的完整分布，但我们认为需要更多分析来完全解释ELECTRA的参数效率。

items.en: Self-Supervised Pre-training for NLP Self-supervised learning has been used to learn word representations (Collobert et al., 2011; Pennington et al., 2014) and more recently contextual representations of words though objectives such as language modeling (Dai & Le, 2015; Peters et al., 2018; Howard & Ruder, 2018). BERT (Devlin et al., 2019) pre-trains a large Transformer (Vaswani et al., 2017) at the masked-language modeling task. There have been numerous extensions to BERT. For example, MASS (Song et al., 2019) and UniLM (Dong et al., 2019) extend BERT to generation tasks by adding auto-regressive generative training objectives. ERNIE (Sun et al., 2019a) and SpanBERT (Joshi et al., 2019) mask out contiguous sequences of token for improved span representations. This idea may be complementary to ELECTRA; we think it would be interesting to make ELECTRA’s generator auto-regressive and add a “replaced span detection” task. Instead of masking out input tokens, XLNet (Yang et al., 2019) masks attention weights such that the input sequence is auto-regressively generated in a random order. However, this method suffers from the same inefficiencies as BERT because XLNet only generates 15% of the input tokens in this way. Like ELECTRA, XLNet may alleviate BERT’s pretrain-finetune discrepancy by not requiring [MASK] tokens, although this isn’t entirely clear because XLNet uses two “streams” of attention during pre-training but only one for fine-tuning. Recently, models such as TinyBERT (Jiao et al., 2019) and MobileBERT (Sun et al., 2019b) show that BERT can effectively be distilled down to a smaller model. In contrast, we focus more on pre-training speed rather than inference speed, so we train ELECTRA-Small from scratch.
items.zh: NLP中的自监督预训练 自监督学习已被用于学习词表示（Collobert等人，2011；Pennington等人，2014），以及最近通过语言建模等目标学习上下文化的词表示（Dai & Le, 2015；Peters等人，2018；Howard & Ruder, 2018）。BERT（Devlin等人，2019）在掩码语言建模任务上预训练了一个大型Transformer（Vaswani等人，2017）。BERT有许多扩展。例如，MASS（Song等人，2019）和UniLM（Dong等人，2019）通过添加自回归生成训练目标将BERT扩展到生成任务。ERNIE（Sun等人，2019a）和SpanBERT（Joshi等人，2019）掩码连续的词元序列以改进跨度表示。这个想法可能与ELECTRA互补；我们认为让ELECTRA的生成器自回归并添加“替换跨度检测”任务会很有趣。XLNet（Yang等人，2019）不是掩码输入词元，而是掩码注意力权重，使得输入序列以随机顺序自回归生成。然而，这种方法存在与BERT相同的低效问题，因为XLNet仅以这种方式生成15%的输入词元。与ELECTRA类似，XLNet可能通过不需要[MASK]词元来缓解BERT的预训练-微调差异，尽管这并不完全清楚，因为XLNet在预训练期间使用两个注意力“流”，但在微调时只使用一个。最近，像TinyBERT（Jiao等人，2019）和MobileBERT（Sun等人，2019b）这样的模型表明，BERT可以有效地蒸馏到更小的模型。相比之下，我们更关注预训练速度而非推理速度，因此我们从零开始训练ELECTRA-Small。

items.en: Generative Adversarial Networks GANs (Goodfellow et al., 2014) are effective at generating high-quality synthetic data. Radford et al. (2016) propose using the discriminator of a GAN in downstream tasks, which is similar to our method. GANs have been applied to text data (Yu et al., 2017; Zhang et al., 2017), although state-of-the-art approaches still lag behind standard maximum-likelihood training (Caccia et al., 2018; Tevet et al., 2018). Although we do not use adversarial learning, our generator is particularly reminiscent of MaskGAN (Fedus et al., 2018), which trains the generator to fill in tokens deleted from the input.
items.zh: 生成对抗网络 GAN（Goodfellow等人，2014）在生成高质量合成数据方面非常有效。Radford等人（2016）提出在下游任务中使用GAN的判别器，这与我们的方法类似。GAN已被应用于文本数据（Yu等人，2017；Zhang等人，2017），尽管最先进的方法仍落后于标准最大似然训练（Caccia等人，2018；Tevet等人，2018）。虽然我们不使用对抗学习，但我们的生成器特别类似于MaskGAN（Fedus等人，2018），后者训练生成器填充从输入中删除的词元。

items.en: Contrastive Learning Broadly, contrastive learning methods distinguish observed data points from fictitious negative samples. They have been applied to many modalities including text (Smith & Eisner, 2005), images (Chopra et al., 2005), and video (Wang & Gupta, 2015; Sermanet et al., 2017) data. Common approaches learn embedding spaces where related data points are similar (Saunshi et al., 2019) or models that rank real data points over negative samples (Collobert et al., 2011; Bordes et al., 2013). ELECTRA is particularly related to Noise-Contrastive Estimation (NCE) (Gutmann & Hyvärinen, 2010), which also trains a binary classifier to distinguish real and fake data points.
items.zh: 对比学习 广义上，对比学习方法将观察到的数据点与虚构的负样本区分开来。它们已被应用于多种模态，包括文本（Smith & Eisner, 2005）、图像（Chopra等人，2005）和视频（Wang & Gupta, 2015；Sermanet等人，2017）数据。常见的方法学习相关数据点相似的嵌入空间（Saunshi等人，2019）或对真实数据点进行排序优于负样本的模型（Collobert等人，2011；Bordes等人，2013）。ELECTRA与噪声对比估计（NCE）（Gutmann & Hyvärinen, 2010）特别相关，后者也训练一个二元分类器来区分真实和虚假的数据点。

items.en: Word2Vec (Mikolov et al., 2013), one of the earliest pre-training methods for NLP, uses contrastive learning. In fact, ELECTRA can be viewed as a massively scaled-up version of Continuous Bag-of-Words (CBOW) with Negative Sampling. CBOW also predicts an input token given surrounding context and negative sampling rephrases the learning task as a binary classification task on whether the input token comes from the data or proposal distribution. However, CBOW uses a bag-of-vectors encoder rather than a transformer and a simple proposal distribution derived from unigram token frequencies instead of a learned generator.
items.zh: Word2Vec (Mikolov 等人, 2013) 作为最早的 NLP 预训练方法之一，使用了对比学习。实际上，ELECTRA 可以被视为带负采样的连续词袋模型（CBOW）的大规模扩展版本。CBOW 也根据周围上下文预测输入词元，而负采样将学习任务重新表述为一个二分类任务，判断输入词元是否来自数据分布或提议分布。然而，CBOW 使用词袋向量编码器而非 Transformer，并且使用基于一元词频的简单提议分布，而非学习到的生成器。

items.en: We have proposed replaced token detection, a new self-supervised task for language representation learning. The key idea is training a text encoder to distinguish input tokens from high-quality negative samples produced by an small generator network. Compared to masked language modeling, our pre-training objective is more compute-efficient and results in better performance on downstream tasks. It works well even when using relatively small amounts of compute, which we hope will make developing and applying pre-trained text encoders more accessible to researchers and practitioners with less access to computing resources. We also hope more future work on NLP pre-training will consider efficiency as well as absolute performance, and follow our effort in reporting compute usage and parameter counts along with evaluation metrics.
items.zh: 我们提出了替换词元检测，这是一种用于语言表示学习的新型自监督任务。其核心思想是训练一个文本编码器，将输入词元与由小型生成器网络产生的高质量负样本区分开来。与掩码语言建模相比，我们的预训练目标在算力上更高效，并在下游任务上取得了更好的性能。即使在算力相对较小的情况下，它也能很好地工作，我们希望这能使预训练文本编码器的开发和应用对算力资源较少的科研人员和从业者更加可及。我们也希望未来更多的 NLP 预训练工作能在关注绝对性能的同时考虑效率，并效仿我们在报告算力使用、参数数量以及评估指标方面的努力。

items.en: We thank Allen Nie, Prajit Ramachandran, audiences at the CIFAR LMB meeting and U. de Montréal, and the anonymous reviewers for their thoughtful comments and suggestions. We thank Matt Peters for answering our questions about ELMo, Alec Radford for answers about GPT, Naman Goyal and Myle Ott for answers about RoBERTa, Zihang Dai for answers about XLNet, Zhenzhong Lan for answers about ALBERT, and Danqi Chen and Mandar Joshi for answers about SpanBERT. Kevin is supported by a Google PhD Fellowship.
items.zh: 我们感谢 Allen Nie、Prajit Ramachandran、CIFAR LMB 会议和蒙特利尔大学的听众，以及匿名审稿人提出的深思熟虑的意见和建议。我们感谢 Matt Peters 回答关于 ELMo 的问题，Alec Radford 回答关于 GPT 的问题，Naman Goyal 和 Myle Ott 回答关于 RoBERTa 的问题，Zihang Dai 回答关于 XLNet 的问题，Zhenzhong Lan 回答关于 ALBERT 的问题，以及 Danqi Chen 和 Mandar Joshi 回答关于 SpanBERT 的问题。Kevin 获得 Google 博士奖学金的支持。
